articles.title: CRYPTO MARKET STRUCTURE BILLS THIS WEEK
articles.summary: Both the Senate Banking and Senate Agriculture Committees have scheduled markup sessions for January 15 to review their respective crypto market structure bills – a critical procedural step where members debate, propose amendments, and vote on advancement. If both bills advance, they would be reconciled into a unified Senate package, sent to the Senate floor, then reconciled with the House-passed CLARITY Act before final votes and presidential signature. Unresolved issues include Americans' rights to earn stablecoin rewards, DeFi sanctions screening requirements at the UI level, and self-custody protections. Banking Committee Chair Tim Scott set January 15 as a firm deadline. Kalshi prediction markets currently assign a 45% probability of passage before May and 66% before 2027.

articles.title: UK CHAIRS PUSH TO BAN CRYPTO POLITICAL DONATIONS
articles.summary: Seven Labour MPs who chair parliamentary committees urged the government to ban cryptocurrency political donations in the upcoming Elections Bill. They believe crypto can obscure fund sources, enable thousands of micro-donations below disclosure thresholds, and raise the risk of foreign interference. The intervention piles pressure on Labour after Reform UK began accepting crypto and comes as the UK tightens its broader crypto regulatory framework.

articles.title: EIP-8004: ROAD TO MAINNET
articles.summary: EIP-8004 proposes establishing Ethereum as a coordination layer for autonomous AI agents through three on-chain registries: identity for agent identification, reputation for tracking behavioral history and trustworthiness, and validation for verifying capabilities and permissions. The specification has progressed from v0.4 to v1.0 as it matures with community input. This standard would enable agents to transact and coordinate with verifiable credentials and reputation systems built directly into Ethereum's base layer.

articles.title: ONCHAIN NEOBANKS TO WATCH IN 2026
articles.summary: Onchain neobanks combine DeFi functionality with traditional banking UX while maintaining self-custody, differentiating from custodial solutions like Coinbase. Three leaders are gaining traction: Superform ($144M TVL and 300% growth since mid-2025) focuses on automated cross-chain yield aggregation across 7+ chains, Veera targets emerging markets with 4M+ users across 108 countries and plans Q1 2026 physical card launch, and Tria leads chain abstraction with 150K users generating $1M daily spend and ~$20M ARR through its invisible multi-chain infrastructure.

articles.title: PAYMENT TOKENIZATION ISN'T ONE-SIZE-FITS-ALL
articles.summary: There are three distinct payment token types: network tokens (Visa/Mastercard, best for recurring payments), Payment service provider tokens (Stripe/Adyen, secure but processor-locked), and digital wallet tokens (Apple Pay/Google Pay, great UX but not the best for subscriptions). Confusing them can lead to vendor lock-in, failed payments, or lost revenue. As payments scale globally, choosing the right tokenization strategy is a growth and reliability decision, not just a security upgrade.

articles.title: HOW I MADE 152% APY ON STABLECOINS
articles.summary: A liquidity provider on ZKP2P generated 152% APY over 9 months by earning spreads on peer-to-peer fiat-to-crypto conversions, producing $12.7K yield on an average $6.3K USDC deposit through 276 capital recycling loops totaling $1.57M in volume. The strategy involved setting above-market rates for USDC sales, receiving fiat payments verified via zero-knowledge proofs, then moving funds back onchain through Monerium and redepositing, with 32% of cycles completing in under 6 hours enabling multiple daily rotations. Market maturation is evident as competition compressed EUR spreads from 2-3% in March 2025 to 0.5-1% currently, while ZKP2P processed over $20M total volume in 2025.

articles.title: HOW I GOT DRAINED AFTER USING PUBLIC HOTEL WI-FI
THE THREE GAMES OF STABLECOIN FINTECH
articles.summary: Building stablecoin-native financial services requires competing on three simultaneous fronts: achieving feature parity with incumbent neobanks like Mercury and Brex, architecting systems that are stablecoin-first from the ground up rather than retrofitting crypto onto legacy infrastructure, and innovating on capabilities that only blockchain rails can enable. There is a unique competitive challenge facing crypto fintech builders, who must meet existing user expectations from traditional fintech while also delivering novel onchain functionality. Success in stablecoin financial services requires both defensive parity plays and offensive innovation beyond what traditional payment rails allow.

articles.title: THREE HARD PROBLEMS FOR DECENTRALIZED STABLECOINS
articles.summary: Decentralized stablecoins must pick an index better than the USD, build oracles that cannot be captured by big money, and resolve staking-yield competition.

articles.title: PRIVACY AND QUANTUM: TWO MEGA NARRATIVES
articles.summary: Privacy (today) and quantum resistance (upcoming) are the two biggest narratives in crypto.

articles.title: ETHEREUM GAS FEES COLLAPSE AS L1 SCALES
articles.summary: Ethereum's average gas fees have fallen below 1 gwei, highlighting how L1 scaling upgrades have materially reduced transaction costs and weakened the practical need for Layer 2s for everyday usage.

articles.title: SOLANA SEEKER SKR TOKEN AIRDROP CONFIRMED
articles.summary: Solana Mobile phone holders will receive 20% of the total supply, with an initial airdrop on January 21.
